title: computed fields without database storage
description: Virtual Elements — Runtime-computed fields without database storage. Key topics: Virtual elements are declared with the `virtual` keyword in front of the. The annotation @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_...'. The exit class must implement interface IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ.

sections.title: Key Learning Points
sections.description:
◆Virtual elements are declared with the `virtual` keyword in front of the
◆The annotation @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_...'
◆The exit class must implement interface IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ
◆Method CALCULATE( ) computes values for the given element(s); the framework
◆Method GET_CALCULATION_INFO( ) provides metadata about which elements
◆Virtual elements are read-only in OData context — they cannot be written back.
◆Virtual elements are supported in RAP projection (C_*) views but NOT in

content:
Key Learning Points

◆Virtual elements are declared with the `virtual` keyword in front of the
◆The annotation @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_...'
◆The exit class must implement interface IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ
◆Method CALCULATE( ) computes values for the given element(s); the framework
◆Method GET_CALCULATION_INFO( ) provides metadata about which elements
◆Virtual elements are read-only in OData context — they cannot be written back.
◆Virtual elements are supported in RAP projection (C_*) views but NOT in
details:
element name inside a CDS projection view.

links the virtual element to an ABAP exit class responsible for computing it.

to receive the runtime callback.

supplies the original data via IO_REQUEST->GET_VALUES( ).

the calculator requires from the database.

basic CDS views (define view) — only in projection views.

PERFORMANCE NOTE

Virtual elements BREAK code pushdown. The ABAP-infrastructure fetches all other

fields from the database first, then invokes the CALCULATE method in the ABAP

layer for EACH row returned. This introduces a round-trip per row group and

prevents the database from optimizing the full query. Use virtual elements

sparingly — prefer CDS expressions, built-in functions, or table functions

whenever the logic can be expressed declaratively in SQL.

CDS — Projection View with Virtual Element
